titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 295
GA 295
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Incipit changed to note by David Parker.
πατρος moved from 6.39 to 6.38 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break change at 1.20 by Rachel Kevern.
τησ changed to της (third one) at 6.1 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break in 12.15 changed by David Parker.
Word break in 6.1 changed by David Parker.
Correction at 5.3 removed and editorial note added by David Parker.
Word breaks corrected at 4.11, 48, 5.5, 18.37 by David Parker.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 302950Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: F189rBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: Susan DochertyTranscription begun: 28th September 2011Transcription finished: 26th February 2012 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - No Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: 4.52 omitted. 5.12 omitted.6.55 omitted. 7.20 omitted.7.53 omitted.16.14 omitted.20.16 omitted.

div.note: There is no incipit, although on F188v there is a list of chapters headedτουκαταιωευαγγελιουκεφαλαια
